zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Savile於1372年出生在英國越可爾坦可斯利時，她的父親Sir John Savile48歲，母親Isabel Eland24歲。她於1389年與John de Ashton在英國英格蘭越可爾坦可斯利結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1410年在英國藍卡爾阿希頓-德-林享年38歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Savile于1372年出生在英国越可爾坦可斯利时，她的父亲Sir John Savile48岁，母亲Isabel Eland24岁。她于1389年与John de Ashton在英国英格兰越可爾坦可斯利结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1410年在英国藍卡爾阿希頓-德-林享年38岁。

nameMeanings.name: Savile
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Saville
meanings: English (of Norman origin; Yorkshire): habitational name from a place in northern France of which the identity is not clear, though it is probably Sainville in Eure-et-Loire, so called from Old French saisne ‘Saxon’ + ville ‘settlement’. Compare Savel , Savell , and Savelle .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Savile was born in 1372, in Tankersley, Yorkshire, England, her father, Sir John Savile, was 48 and her mother, Isabel Eland, was 24. She married John de Ashton in 1389, in Tankersley, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. She died in 1410, in Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, England, at the age of 38.

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Savile(이)가 1372년 영국 요르크실르 탠커슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Savile(은)는 48세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Eland(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1389년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 탠커슬리에서 John de Ashton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1410년 영국 랭카실르 애슈토-운더-리니에서 38세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Ashton Under Lyne, Lancashire, England: аштон ундир лин, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Savile родилась в 1372 г. в местности танкирсли, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Savile было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Eland, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de Ashton в 1389 г. в местности танкирсли, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в 1410 г. в местности аштон-ундир-лин, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 38 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Savile nació en 1372, en Tankersley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Savile, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Isabel Eland, tenía 24 años. Se casó con John de Ashton en 1389, en Tankersley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en 1410, en Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 38 años.
